student_name: Eliane Portela Nogueira Lima de Brito
hub_subtitle: Business English for a doctor rebuilding her professional voice

lesson.menu_title: Making It Land — Speaking with Emphasis and Impact
lesson.menu_desc: Emphatic inversion (Never before have we grown this fast; Not only did we open a clinic, but we also broke into a new market) to deliver milestones so they land -- 31 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 19 -- Making It Land
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Eliane Portela | Aula 19 -- Making It Land: Speaking with Emphasis and Impact

listenings.text: Eliane, it is Julian. I listened to the draft of your keynote, and I want to push you to make it land. Right now the content is strong, but the delivery understates it. Here is the thing: never before have you had a story this good, so do not bury it. Open with a hook. Not only should you state the milestone, but you should also pause for effect and let it hit home. When you get to the big number, drive it home with conviction. Rarely does an audience remember your data. They remember the one soundbite that carried gravitas. And end with a clear call to action. Only when you speak as if every word matters will the room truly listen. You have earned this, Eliane. Now go and command the room.

Good evening. I want to begin with one number. Two years ago, this clinic did not exist. Never before had our city had a center like this one, and not only did we open it, but we did it against the odds. Let me be clear about what that means. Only after countless setbacks did the first breakthrough come. No sooner had we found our footing than the demand doubled. I will not understate it. This has been the hardest and the most important work of my life. So here is my call to action. Rarely do we get to build something that outlasts us. This is that chance. Join us, and let us make the next chapter land even harder than the first.
